title: Add possibility to completely disable cgroup management
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Our organization has a use-case for podman where we need it to not manage cgroups. Podman switches cgroup when it's setting up rootless environment, so setting `--cgroups disabled` doesn't prevent this as it is a command flag (and rootless env is being set up in the root part of program). So we'd like an option that completely disables any and all cgroup management that podman does. This issue has been discussed in this PR - https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7721 and we've come to the conclusion that the best/cleanest approach to solve this would probably be to add `disabled` option to `--cgroup-manager` which would automatically also set `--cgroups disabled` for all commands.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I think that the approach could solve the issue.

@mheon are you fine with it?
I'm perfectly fine with this - makes sense from my perspective.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe could you create a PR to make this happen?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe Did you ever get a chance to look at this?

not yet.

@zv0n would accepting the cgroup manager through an environment variable work for you (e.g. `CGROUP_MANAGER=disabled`)?
@giuseppe We've done some testing and it seems that `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/dev/zero` works, thanks for the idea!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe @zv0n is this still something we need  to fix.

The plumbing is not trivial and there is a workaround.

Let's close it.
